projectDescription: A self-hosted HTTP proxy that removes CORS headers, modifies HTML, and simulates different client environments to help developers test and analyze web content delivery.
keyFeatures: HTTP proxy that removes and modifies CORS headers
Domain-based ruleset system for custom request/response modifications
HTML injection and regex-based content rewriting
Custom User-Agent and X-Forwarded-For header spoofing
Basic Auth protection for public instances
Docker, Helm, and binary deployment options
Domain allowlist for restricting proxy usage
Ruleset sharing and merging between instances
publicApiSurface: GET /{url} — Proxy a website through Ladder with HTML rewriting
GET /api/{url} — Fetch a site and return JSON with headers and body
POST /api — Fetch a site via JSON body request
GET /raw/{url} — Fetch raw HTML without any rewriting
GET /ruleset — Expose the current loaded ruleset as YAML
--ruleset / -r — Path, directory, or URL to a ruleset YAML
--merge-rulesets — Compile a directory of YAML files into a single ruleset
--merge-rulesets-gzip — Compile rulesets into a gzip-compressed file
